title: Memory Corruption & Drift
concept: Identifying when an agent consolidates incorrect facts or outdated beliefs, and correction strategies.

exercises.prompt: What helps an AI agent efficiently find relevant memories?
exercises.explain: Metadata filtering helps an AI organize and quickly access relevant memories.

cards.title: Memory Corruption & Drift
cards.body: Sometimes, AI agents remember things that are wrong or outdated. This can make their decisions less accurate.

cards.title: What is Memory Corruption?
cards.body: Memory corruption happens when an agent holds incorrect information. This can lead to mistakes in its behavior or responses.

cards.title: Think of a Library
cards.body: Imagine a library that has old books mixed with new. If a librarian only treats the old books as valid, the information shared will be incorrect or outdated.

cards.title: Identifying Memory Drift
cards.body: If an agent insists that a smartphone was invented in 2005 when it was actually released in 2007, that’s an indication of memory drift.

cards.title: Myth: AI Never Forget Facts
cards.body: It's a myth that AI always remembers facts accurately. Just like humans, AI can forget or misremember.

cards.title: Spotting Memory Issues
cards.body: Watch for signs like inconsistencies in responses or outdated info. Detecting these helps in fixing memory errors.

left.text: Inconsistent answers may indicate memory issues.

exercises.prompt: What should an agent do if it realizes it has incorrect information?

options.text: Update its memory with the correct information
Ask for help from another agent

exercises.explain: Updating memory with correct information ensures that the agent improves its knowledge and accuracy.

exercises.prompt: Agents can have outdated beliefs just like humans do.
exercises.explain: This is true; both agents and humans can hold onto outdated or incorrect memories.

exercises.prompt: Memory drift leads to ______ in an AI agent's performance.
exercises.sentence: Memory drift leads to a decline in an AI agent's performance.
exercises.explain: A decline in performance means the agent isn’t making accurate decisions anymore.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about memory corruption is correct?

options.text: It helps the AI make better decisions.
It always results in correct answers.
It can lead to inconsistent behavior.

exercises.explain: Memory corruption can cause an agent to act unpredictably, leading to inconsistency.

exercises.prompt: What actions can an AI agent take to correct memory corruption?

options.text: Ignore any incorrect memories and trust its feelings.
Consolidate information and update from reliable sources.
Delete all existing memories and start fresh.

exercises.explain: The best way to correct memory corruption is for an agent to consolidate its knowledge, ensuring it learns from accurate and reliable sources.
